target.name: Neuralink

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Scale AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $29B

data.narrative:
Neuralink is a private-stage AI & Software company that has raised $1.3B+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a fragmented peer universe: one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private peers including Scale AI, Waymo, Figure AI, and Reflection AI. However, the sector contains 16 total AI & Software peers, with insufficient overlap in stage, adjacency, and valuation tier to support a robust peer-median approach.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The analysis identified only one same-stage private comparable and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier peers meeting minimum thresholds (≥3 datapoints required for statistical reliability). Widening filters to all 16 sector peers would introduce excessive heterogeneity across development stage and scale, rendering results unreliable for a frontier-stage company of Neuralink's capital intensity and technological scope.

Without sufficient narrow comparables or triangulated valuation outputs, this analysis cannot responsibly project a specific valuation range. Key uncertainties include the absence of near-peer private financings at comparable scale, the nascent nature of the neurotechnology commercialization landscape, and the lack of revenue or clinical-stage benchmarks in the dataset. A credible valuation would require either a successful Series round establishing market-tested pricing, expansion of comparable data from later-stage peers, or alternative methodologies anchored to development milestones and addressable market size.
